FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Class ContentDialog

Namespace
FluentGpu.Controls
Assembly
FluentGpu.Controls.dll

WinUI-style ContentDialog: a modal, page-level smoke scrim with a centered solid card. The dialog is hosted through OverlayHost instead of being drawn inside the sample card, so it overlays the whole page.

1:1 with ContentDialog_themeresources.xaml:

  • Chrome — smoke #4D000000 (SmokeFillColorDefault), background SolidBackgroundFillColorBase, 1px SurfaceStrokeColorDefault border, OverlayCornerRadius 8, dialog padding 24 (ContentDialogPadding), title margin 0,0,0,12 (ContentDialogTitleMargin), button spacing 8 (ContentDialogButtonSpacing), separator CardStrokeColorDefault 0,0,0,1, Min/Max 320×184 / 548×756 (:12-15).
  • Motion — open: scale 1.05→1.0 over ControlNormal (250ms) + opacity 0→1 over ControlFaster (83ms, linear); close: scale 1.0→1.05 over ControlFast (167ms) + opacity 1→0 over 83ms; both scale legs on the ControlFastOutSlowInKeySpline (0,0,0,1) — ContentDialog_themeresources.xaml:77-117 DialogShowing/Hidden transitions, driven by the OverlayHost's Modal chrome.
  • Keyboard — Enter invokes the DefaultButton (the accent-styled one); Escape closes with None (the overlay's Escape preview); Tab/Shift-Tab CYCLE inside the dialog (DialogShowing sets BackgroundElement.TabFocusNavigation=Cycle, :123 — a REAL dispatcher focus scope via the host's PopupOptions.FocusTrap). Initial focus lands on the default button (TabIndex ranks it first).
public sealed class ContentDialog : Component

Fields

CloseClick 

public Action? CloseClick

Field Value

Action

CloseText 

public string CloseText

Field Value

string

Closed

Raised once per close with the WinUI ContentDialogResult (the ShowAsync return value): Primary/Secondary for the commands, None for the close button, Escape, or a programmatic close.

public Action<ContentDialogResult>? Closed

Field Value

Action<ContentDialogResult>

DefaultButton 

public ContentDialog.DefaultBtn DefaultButton

Field Value

ContentDialog.DefaultBtn

Message 

public string Message

Field Value

string

OpenOnMount 

public bool OpenOnMount

Field Value

bool

PartCommandRow

The footer command row (WinUI CommandSpace) — built only when a button is shown. Owned: Children (the command buttons: their click handlers, default-button accent, and TabIndex focus-trap ranking).

public const string PartCommandRow = "CommandRow"

Field Value

string

PartContent

The padded content region above the separator (the WinUI DialogSpace top overlay). Owned: Children (the title + message structure).

public const string PartContent = "Content"

Field Value

string

PartPlate

The dialog card (WinUI BackgroundElement): fill, border, corners, shadow, min/max sizes… Owned: OnKeyDown (Enter → default button, chained with any modifier-supplied handler), Children (the content · separator · command-row structure).

public const string PartPlate = "Plate"

Field Value

string

PartTitle

The title text (the WinUI Title presenter) — a TextEl part: customize via Parts.Set<TextEl>(ContentDialog.PartTitle, t => t with { … }). Owned: none (pure styling).

public const string PartTitle = "Title"

Field Value

string

Parts

Lightweight per-part styling (CSS ::part): modifiers keyed by the PartXxx consts; see TemplateParts for the contract.

public TemplateParts? Parts

Field Value

TemplateParts

FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
